summary:
• Iran geopolitical tensions are creating significant supply risk with analysts projecting oil could surge to $180 if U.S.-Iran conflict escalates, while Persian Gulf tanker traffic faces structural headwinds that may permanently reduce regional export capacity.

• LNG supply chains are tightening globally with China's import recovery ahead of peak cooling season and Canada's phase two expansion approval, while Australian facility strikes add near-term supply concerns that could support elevated LNG pricing.

• Ukraine's drone strikes on Russian refineries combined with France's enforcement actions against Russian oil tankers are disrupting traditional supply routes and creating structural constraints on global fuel supply that Goldman Sachs expects will sustain strong refining margins through 2026.

articles.title: Persian Gulf Oil Tanker Traffic May Never Fully Recover
articles.description: Oil and gas traffic via the Strait of Hormuz may never go back to pre-war levels if Iran cements its hold over the chokepoint. The warnings come from several unrelated sources as the war continues to drag on, with some recovery in traffic but nowhere near pre-February 28 levels.
